arts.ttl: Acerbi suffers muscle elongation, likely to miss Juventus clash
arts.sum: Inter Milan’s defensive woes have deepened as Francesco Acerbi joins Hakan Calhanoglu on the injury list following the team’s victory against Roma. The Italian defender underwent medical examinations today after experiencing muscular discomfort during the first half of the match at the Olimpico, and the results have confirmed the worst fears of the coaching staff. Acerbi was forced to leave the field in the 24th minute after a challenge with Dovbyk in midfield. He attempted to stretch the biceps femoris muscle in his left thigh on the pitch but was immediately replaced by De Vrij. arts.ttl: UCL Liveblog: Milan-Club Brugge, Juventus-VfB, Aston Villa-Bologna
Timothy Weah opens up about Juventus, Inter rivalry, and teammate bonds
arts.sum: In a candid interview with The Athletic, Juventus forward Timothy Weah shared his thoughts on the team’s new playing style, his relationship with teammates, and the rivalry with Inter Milan. Discussing Juventus under Thiago Motta, Weah said, “It’s more positive, more attacking, more possession-based. I feel we can definitely score more, and that goes for me too, I can definitely score more, but I think we’re doing very well this season. If you look at most of the games we play, we don’t concede, we dominate, and we score as well. This season is really fun. arts.ttl: Juventus reach agreement to extend contracts of two first team players – report
arts.sum: Juventus have reportedly reached agreements to extend Mattia Perin and Federico Gatti’s contracts. Two Juventus...

arts.ttl: Juventus secure contract extensions for Perin and Gatti
arts.sum: Juventus is set to strengthen its squad stability by finalizing contract renewals for goalkeeper Mattia Perin and defender Federico Gatti. The club has reached agreements with both players, with only the official signatures remaining to complete the deals. Calciomercato report that for Perin, the day is particularly special as he prepares to start in goal for Juventus against Stuttgart in the UEFA Champions League. The 31-year-old keeper will step in for the suspended Michele Di Gregorio, who was sent off in the previous match against Leipzig. According to reports, Juventus and Perin’s representatives have agreed on a two-year contract extension. The post Juventus secure contract extensions for Perin and Gatti appeared first on FootItalia.com.
